ops.title: Fresh-epoch one-shot promotion for a refrozen eval cutover
ops.body:
## `--fresh-epoch`: one-shot clean-slate promotion (commit e246a077)

### Why it exists
When the frozen promotion eval is wiped and refrozen clean-slate, the first
clean-data retrain must NOT be gated against incumbents whose training data AND
stored eval probs came from the corrupted era. `--fresh-epoch` gates the
candidate "as if no incumbent existed." It is a deliberate one-shot cutover
flag, not a reusable skip-the-gate switch.

### Watcher lane (`retrain_watcher`, Option A)
- Skips `_load_incumbent_probs` + the incumbent-relative `corrected_gate` /
  `watcher_promotable` entirely.
- Keeps every already-absolute check: spend cap and mlx parity. These are NOT
  incumbent-relative, so they still run.
- Adds exactly one absolute refusal: candidate sentinel AUC on the clean frame
  must beat 0.5. `<= 0.5` is below chance → raises `WatcherRetrainError`
  ("below chance"). This is a degenerate-state crash, NOT a quality gate — 0.5
  is the canonical no-skill anchor, not a calibrated threshold. There is
  deliberately no AUC *floor* above 0.5 (the whole point is we have no
  trustworthy reference yet).
- One-shot guard `FreshEpochError` (`_refuse_scored_frame`): before training,
  refuses if ANY registered version already carries probs whose stored
  `dataset_digest` == the current frame digest ("the frame already has scored
  incumbents; the cutover is over"). Names the offending probs file(s).
- Registration, prune, `write_probs`, kickstart are unchanged. The serving
  model stays live until the candidate registers over it. `should_retrain` is
  unchanged, so the next weekly pass short-circuits on the recorded digest.
- Journal line: `fresh-epoch promoted <ver> (candidate AUC X, no incumbent
  gate); ...`.
- `incumbent is None` no longer hard-raises under fresh-epoch (fresh-epoch
  doesn't need one); the raise is `incumbent is None and not fresh_epoch`.

### Gate lane (`retrain_gate`)
- Routes through `gate_promotable`'s EXISTING no-incumbent path by passing
  `incumbent_metrics=None` (`incumbent is None or fresh_epoch`). This is in-code
  precedent, not invented semantics. Journal-marked `fresh-epoch promoted ...`.
- The gate lane keeps NO probs store (it compares registry-metadata metrics, not
  `load_probs`/`probs_path`), so there is NO reuse guard on this lane. The
  `--fresh-epoch` help text documents that the guard is watcher-only.

### Why NOT the plan's original framing
The T2 plan spoke of "budget-held + coverage sign-test" as absolute fresh-epoch
checks and assumed a no-incumbent absolute bar to reuse. That was wrong about
this code: budget_held and coverage are incumbent-relative by construction in
`corrected_gate`, and the watcher's only real no-incumbent path is the ungated
`register_watcher` (register a pre-built adapter, no training, no bar). So
Option A skips the relative gate and adds the single below-chance crash instead.

### CLI wiring
`--fresh-epoch` is a flag on `cc-steer retrain`, threaded keyword-only into both
`retrain_gate(fresh_epoch=...)` and `retrain_watcher(fresh_epoch=...)`. It is
rejected in combination with the watcher bootstrap paths
(`--seed-incumbent-probs`, `--register-adapter`), which don't train/gate.
ops.when: When modifying retrain promotion logic (promotion.py, lexical.retrain_gate, watcher.retrain_watcher), using or changing the --fresh-epoch flag, or reasoning about how the first clean-data retrain bypasses corrupted-era incumbents
